mnemonics.title: Three Pillars of Green IT
mnemonics.content: EE-GE-WR: Energy Efficiency, Green Energy, Waste Reduction (Think: Eco-Friendly = E-E-G-E-WR)

mnemonics.title: Optimal Server Utilization
mnemonics.content: 70-85%: Think '70-85% is efficient, like a well-run factory'

acronyms.usage: Measures overall data center energy efficiency; lower is better (ideal = 1.0)
Measures carbon emissions per unit of energy consumed; key for environmental impact assessment
Measures water consumption for cooling; important for sustainability in water-scarce regions
Power-saving technique that adjusts CPU voltage/frequency based on workload
Isolated computing environment running on physical hardware; enables consolidation and energy savings

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circle: Inner circle = IT Energy, Outer circle = Total Energy. PUE = Outer/Inner. Closer circles = Better PUE (more efficient)
Imagine boxes (VMs) being moved from many small trucks (servers) to fewer big trucks, saving fuel (energy)
Traffic light analogy: Green = Full Power/Active, Yellow = Idle, Red = Sleep/Off. Want most lights green when working, red when not needed

analogies.analogy: PUE is like miles-per-gallon for a car: lower numbers mean better efficiency and less waste
Like moving from many small apartments to one big house - less building maintenance, heating, and overhead overall
A 10% utilized server is like a bus with only 1 passenger - expensive per person. 80% utilization is like a full bus - efficient
Like doing laundry when electricity is cheapest (at night) but also when it's cleanest (less coal power on grid)
